findings.title: Under-defined hash taxonomy for content vs payload
findings.description: The manifest defines content_hash as normalized rendered content, but payload_hash (used for live/batch equivalence and model-consumable payloads) lacks a byte-scope definition. This makes hashes incomparable across different builders.
findings.fix: Define a strict hash taxonomy with explicit byte-scopes for source_hash, block_hash, content_hash, payload_hash, and output_hash.

findings.title: Missing failure rule for budget vs protected blocks
findings.description: PreambleBlock is protected from truncation, but the system also enforces hard budgets. There is no defined behavior for when protected blocks alone exceed the budget.
findings.fix: Extend the packet build contract to include an explicit 'budget_exceeded' or 'unrenderable_within_budget' failure outcome.

findings.title: Phase ordering and test criteria sequencing inconsistency
findings.description: Phase 1 exit criteria include equivalence tests for overview payloads and plan-close artifacts, but the migration for those components does not occur until Phase 2 and Phase 4.
findings.fix: Move component-specific equivalence tests to the phases where those components are actually migrated.

findings.title: Inconsistent token budget metadata in dispatch profiles
findings.description: The plan relies on profile-aware token budgets, but profiles in 'shared/llm_dispatch.py' inconsistently define context-window limits, often conflating them with output-token limits.
findings.fix: Standardize dispatch profiles to explicitly expose context-window limits separate from output limits.

findings.title: Unspecified handling of non-text source entities
findings.description: The plan requires test coverage for binary files, symlinks, and submodules, but no normative behavior or placeholder policy is defined for these types.
findings.fix: Create a source-entity policy matrix specifying exact rendering and manifest behavior for text, binary, deleted, symlink, and submodule entities.

findings.title: Ambiguous equivalence target for Overview live/batch
findings.description: Current live paths prepend freshness metadata to final outputs, making byte-for-byte equivalence with batch mode impossible unless the 'payload' scope is strictly isolated.
findings.fix: Introduce a single Python API that returns the exact prompt payload artifact and require both live and batch wrappers to call it.

findings.title: Lack of automated enforcement against helper duplication
findings.description: Success criteria prohibit duplicated helpers, but enforcement is described as manual or test-based rather than using machine-checkable AST or import boundary gates.
findings.fix: Implement CI-level grep or AST rules (e.g., preventing 'parse_file_spec' definitions outside 'shared/file_specs.py').

findings.title: Domain-specific logic leak in shared preamble helper
findings.description: The 'context_preamble.py' module includes constitution and agent-economics text assembly, which is semantically specialized for reviews rather than being a generic packet mechanic.
findings.fix: Isolate mechanical preamble assembly from task-specific semantic content.

findings.title: Incomplete selector precedence rules for migration
findings.description: Legacy plan-close code has implicit precedence (e.g., --file overrides touched-file discovery) that is not recorded in the migration plan, risking silent drift.
findings.fix: Explicitly document and formalize the precedence of selector rules (explicit files vs git ranges vs worktree).

findings.title: Low-value CLI surface expansion
findings.description: The proposed 'scripts/context-packet.py' adds a public surface and compatibility burden that may be unnecessary if importable builders are the primary integration mode.
findings.fix: Deprioritize or remove the generic CLI unless specifically required by shell-facing consumers.
